summary: 60 transactions sent across 5 fresh users (sim-p2-4985-u1 through u5). Intended mix: 49 LOW, 7 MEDIUM, 4 HIGH. The baseline phase (5 txns/user posted in rapid succession) triggered a velocity anomaly heuristic on txn #5 for every user — all 5 users were frozen before any MEDIUM or HIGH transaction could process. Result: 20 LOW classified, 5 LOW-intended classified as HIGH (velocity trigger), 35 rejected with HTTP 423 (frozen account). No MEDIUM or HIGH transactions were evaluated by the fraud engine. The $5000 absolute threshold was never tested. The velocity check (5 txns in 5 minutes) is a more aggressive trigger than documented and fires even on legitimate small-value transactions submitted in batch, creating severe false-positive risk.

brief_for_next: Phase 3 edge-case-hunter: five users sim-p2-4985-u1 through sim-p2-4985-u5 exist in the DB but ALL are frozen. You must use fresh user IDs (different prefix). Key behaviors confirmed: (1) velocity heuristic triggers HIGH+freeze at exactly 5 txns in 5 minutes regardless of amount — the threshold appears to be >=5 txns in a 5-minute window; (2) once frozen, all subsequent transactions are HTTP 423 rejected and never touch the fraud engine; (3) the $5000 absolute HIGH trigger was NOT testable in this run due to premature freezing — edge-case-hunter should test it with a fresh user sending just 1-2 baseline txns then immediately one at $4999, $5000, $5001; (4) self-contamination bug from Phase 1 means ratio-based 30x trigger is unreliable — stick to absolute $5000 threshold; (5) API requires header X-API-Key: sentinel-dev-key; (6) backend is in heuristic mode (no valid DeepSeek key). DB has transactions from sim-p2-4985-* prefix. Use a new prefix like sim-p3-<rand>.
